title: lithocnides lappulacea
intro: Lithocnides lappulacea, also known as Lithocarpus, is a type of deciduous tree native to East Asia, particularly in China and Japan, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. Lithocarpus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Lithocarpus leaves and bark are commonly used in herbal preparations, including tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
